name: Seftel 2009 Tadalafil ED Psychosocial Outcomes Extract
population_or_context: Men with erectile dysfunction after a 4-week run-in; not a healthy enhancement population.
sample_size: 342 randomized: tadalafil 5 mg once daily N=264; placebo N=78.
methods: Randomized placebo-controlled 12-week tadalafil 5 mg once-daily ED efficacy study with sexual satisfaction and psychosocial outcomes.
population_profile: Men with erectile dysfunction; indirect for mostly healthy men age 30 to 50 because the psychosocial endpoints were measured in an ED treatment context.
tadalafil_exposure: Tadalafil 5 mg once daily for 12 weeks.
indication_context: On-label erectile-dysfunction treatment context; used here only as indirect mood/well-being and quality-of-life evidence.
study_design: Randomized placebo-controlled parallel trial with 12-week treatment after placebo run-in.
safety_findings: Abstract does not provide detailed adverse-event extraction; interpret alongside the published tadalafil safety baseline and current labeling.
directness_to_target_population: Indirect: tadalafil-specific human evidence, but population had ED and outcomes were tightly tied to sexual function and relationship context.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Psychosocial and satisfaction improvements correlated with erectile-function improvement, limiting inference about independent mood or well-being effects.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence; no cross-agent transfer, but endpoint and population transfer to healthy mood/well-being claims is weak because benefits were measured in ED-related psychosocial instruments.
